name: Canggu - Batu Bolong (峇里島 - 巴杜柏隆 / Batu Bolong)
distance_from_dps_airport_km: 20 km (approx. 60-75 min by car depending on traffic)
distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: 0-5 min (in town); From Uluwatu: 60-90 min by scooter
wave_type: Beach break with flat reef bottom, predominantly left and right peaks, mellow and forgiving (soft rolling shoulders, point-break-like long rides on the inside)
skill_level: Beginner to Intermediate (also a longboarder favorite)
wave_height_range: 2-6 ft (knee to chest high typical; can hold up to ~8 ft on bigger days)
best_season: Dry season May-October produces the most consistent swell; shoulder seasons (Nov-Dec, Mar-Apr) often have cleaner glassy mornings with lighter winds. Wet season (Nov-Mar) still rideable but with onshore westerlies and poorer water quality after rain.
best_tide: Mid to high tide (mid-incoming is the sweet spot; low tide exposes reef and makes the wave dumpy/shallow)
best_wind: Offshore from the East / Northeast (SE-E trade winds in dry season are ideal; wind typically turns onshore around 10-11am)
best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) primary; also works on S and SSW
swell_consistency_rating: 5/5 (one of the most consistent breaks in Bali, rideable almost every day of the year)
wave_length_meters: 50-150 m on a good set (long peeling shoulders, especially favorable for longboarding) [uncertain]
wave_speed_rating: Slow (soft and forgiving, ideal for longboards and learners)
takeoff_zone_difficulty: Easy - wide takeoff zone, gentle drop, no steep ledge; main difficulty is navigating heavy crowd traffic rather than the wave itself
secondary_peaks: Multiple peaks along the beach; an inside reform section provides extra rides toward shore. Old Man's peak to the north and Nelayan/Pererenan further north offer adjacent alternatives.
peak_crowd_level: 5/5 - extremely crowded, one of the busiest lineups in Bali. Peak crowd 8am-11am and 3pm-sunset. Heaviest in July-August and December-January (high season).
access: Walk directly onto the beach from Jalan Pantai Batu Bolong; multiple beach access points via cafes (Old Man's, La Brisa, The Lawn). No stairs or cave entry required.
paddle_out_method: Walk in from the sandy beach; paddle straight out through the whitewater. Easiest paddle-out in Canggu - no channel navigation needed.
entry_fee: No beach entry fee. Parking: ~IDR 5,000 for car, IDR 2,000-3,000 for scooter at official lots.
recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30am) for glassy, less crowded conditions; sunset session also popular for vibe and lighting.
best_time_of_day: Before 7am to avoid the surf-school rush and onshore wind; mid-week mornings significantly less crowded than weekends.
nearby_warung: Warung Bu Mi (cheap local Indonesian), Old Man's Restaurant, La Brisa, The Lawn, Betelnut Cafe, Cafe Vida, Sand Bar; coconut stalls and warungs line Jalan Pantai Batu Bolong.
alternative_spot_within_10min: Old Man's (immediately north, ~2 min walk), Nelayan Beach (~5 min), Berawa (~10 min south), Echo Beach (~10 min north).
hazards: Heavy crowd (collision risk with loose longboards is the #1 hazard), shallow reef at low tide, occasional sea urchins on the reef, river-mouth runoff/poor water quality after heavy rain (skin infections, ear infections), beginners ditching boards.
marine_hazards: Sea urchins (urchin spines on reef - avoid low tide; remove with vinegar/tweezers, see clinic if deep). Occasional small jellyfish in wet season. No regular shark or stonefish reports. Bacterial water issues post-rainfall can cause ear/skin infections.
reef_cut_severity_index: Low to medium - reef is relatively flat and not razor-sharp like Uluwatu/Padang. Minor scrapes most common; deep cuts rare. Higher risk only at low tide. [uncertain]
rip_current_pattern: Moderate rips form between sandbar sections and near the small river mouth on the north side; visible as darker, calmer channels. Strongest on outgoing tide and bigger swells. Generally manageable for intermediate paddlers. [uncertain]
lineup_etiquette: Standard surf etiquette applies but rarely enforced due to crowd density. Priority to surfer closest to peak; expect frequent drop-ins. Smile and say hi, don't snake locals, give right of way to longboarders setting up early. Surf-school groups dominate the inside.
localism_intensity: Low - one of the most welcoming and tourist-friendly lineups in Bali. Locals generally tolerant though paddle-battles for sets do happen.
respect_protocol: Be courteous to surf-school students and instructors who teach the inside daily; don't drop in on Indonesian locals; rinse off respectfully near temple/offering areas on the beach; cover up when walking through beachside warungs.
resident_local_legends: Local Canggu/Pererenan crew including longboarders associated with Deus Ex Machina 'Nine Foot and Single' culture (e.g., Made Lana, Flynn Novak visits); regular pro and shaper presence. [uncertain]
religious_sensitivity: Pura Batu Bolong (small Hindu sea temple on the cliffed rock outcrop at the south end of the beach) is an active worship site - do not climb on offerings (canang sari), women on menstruation traditionally should not enter temple areas. Respect Nyepi (Day of Silence, typically March) - no surfing, beach closed.
medical_nearby: Penta Medical Canggu (24h clinic on Jl. Raya Canggu), Canggu Medical Clinic, BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (major), Siloam Hospital Denpasar; reef cuts treated locally with iodine + antibiotics.
nearest_clinic_distance_km: Penta Medical Canggu ~1-2 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~25 km; Siloam Denpasar ~15 km
rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski; informal lifeguard presence from Balawista on busy days; surf schools maintain on-water instructor support.
emergency_contact: Bali Emergency: 112; Balawista lifeguards: +62 361 755660; Police: 110; Ambulance: 118
nearest_surf_school: Numerous surf schools cluster on Batu Bolong beach: Dian Surf School, Surf Well Bali, Sea N Surf, Surf With Aldo, Pro Surf School Bali, Odyssey Surf School. Most operate directly on the sand with lessons starting at IDR 350,000-450,000.
board_rental_availability: Abundant - dozens of board rental stalls along the beach (longboards, foamies, mid-lengths, shortboards)
surf_photographer_available: Yes - multiple land-based photographers and water-photographers shoot daily at Batu Bolong; photos available via Instagram/QR code on the beach (typically delivered same day).
photographer_recommendation: Local beach photographers operate on commission; typical price IDR 150,000-300,000 for a session pack of edited photos. Notable: @canggusurfphoto, @batubolongphoto-style operators. [uncertain]
accommodation_options: Wide range: budget hostels (The Farm Hostel, Gypsy Moon), mid-range guesthouses (Serenity Eco Guesthouse), surf camps (Padang Padang Surf Camp Canggu, Odyssey Surf Camp), boutique hotels (COMO Uma Canggu, The Slow). Walking distance to break highly available.
webcam_url: https://www.webcamtaxi.com/en/indonesia/bali/canggu-sandbar-cam.html (Sand Bar live cam); https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/old-mans-batu-bolong/605112930a374f33cf5f8f05 (Surfline Old Mans - Batu Bolong)
drone_allowed: Generally tolerated but technically requires PASI / DGCA registration for drones >250g in Indonesia; avoid flying over temple, crowded lineup, or low altitude over swimmers. Many local photographers fly drones daily.
coworking_nearby: Yes - Outpost Canggu (Jl. Tanah Barak), Tropical Nomad (Jl. Pantai Berawa), B Work Bali (Jl. Nelayan), Dojo Bali (Echo Beach); all 5-15 min scooter ride.
avg_lesson_price: IDR 400,000-500,000 (~USD 25-32 / TWD 800-1,000) per 2-hour group lesson; private lessons IDR 600,000-900,000
board_rental_daily: Half-day (4h): IDR 75,000-150,000; full-day: IDR 100,000-200,000 depending on board type (longboard typically higher)
water_quality_rating: Dry season (May-Oct): Good to Fair. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Poor after heavy rain due to river-mouth runoff and drainage discharging directly into lineup; risk of ear/skin/stomach infection. Avoid surfing for 24-48h after heavy downpour.
annual_competition_window: Hosts regional/longboard contests sporadically (e.g., Deus 9ft & Single Festival held annually around August-September at nearby Pererenan/Canggu beaches). No major WSL CT event. [uncertain]
community_events: Deus Sunday surf jams, 'Old Man's Wednesday' style sessions, Single Fin Sunday (informal), beach clean-ups by 4Ocean and Trash Hero Canggu; sunset DJ sessions at La Brisa and The Lawn.
